parsed_review.summary: CP00-542 is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the RP17 AI Governance series (P07 permission fixture slice: M05 Permission And Audit Binding tail + M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head, 10 units, risk A). All layers are consistent: closeout-pack-plan.json, manifest, contract current_pack/p07_permission_fixture_slice_descriptor, registry PACK_BINDING/REQUIREMENTS/NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION, service.js factories, validators.js, and the validate script's deepEqual contract guards all agree on pack_id, risk_class, unit_count=10, range RP17.P07.M05.S21-RP17.P07.M06.S05, deliverable split (claude_review:1/implementation:4/failure_recovery:5), micro-title split (5/5), upstream CP00-541, and next CP00-543/RP17.P07.M06.S06. The descriptor chain correctly references AiGovernanceCoreCp541P07WorkflowPermissionSliceDescriptor. Three substantive CP542 tests assert coverage, descriptor-only row guards, and authority boundaries. The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly held at false (runtime_ready false) with no overclaim. Authority boundaries are enforced in code and request/baseline: Claude is read_only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false), no enterprise trust from local validation, human approval required for runtime opening. Hermes is used only for the deterministic descriptor evidence packet (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false), not product runtime. No real data (synthetic_only, real_client_data_loaded=false) and exhaustive no-write/no-dispatch flags. Review hardening evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON, 1455B), tools limited to Read/Grep/Glob with no forbidden tools, prompt 2177B within the 25600B limit, summary-only prohibited. command-evidence.json records the full validation suite at exit 0, and code-level consistency supports that those gates pass. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
